title: Color Enum to String Converter
prompt_md:
## Goal
Convert a list of numeric color codes into their string names using a `constexpr` enum-to-string function.

Color codes: `0` = red, `1` = green, `2` = blue. Any other value is `unknown`.

## Input
- Line 1: an integer `n` (1 <= n <= 10), the number of codes.
- Next `n` lines: each contains a single integer color code (may be out of range).

## Output
Exactly `n` lines, each the string name of the corresponding color (`red`, `green`, `blue`, or `unknown`).

## Constraints
- Define an unscoped `enum Color` and write a `constexpr std::string_view` function (using a `switch`) that maps an enumerator to its name. Convert each code with `static_cast`.

title: Day-of-Week Round Trip Converter
prompt_md:
## Goal
Convert day-of-week names to their numeric values and back, demonstrating both string-to-enum and enum-to-string conversion.

Valid day names (all lowercase): `monday` (0), `tuesday` (1), `wednesday` (2), `thursday` (3), `friday` (4), `saturday` (5), `sunday` (6).

## Input
- Line 1: an integer `n` (1 <= n <= 10), the number of queries.
- Next `n` lines: each contains a single lowercase word (a day name or some other string).

## Output
For each query print one line:
- If the word is a valid day name: `<day_name> = <value>` (the canonical name produced by enum-to-string, and the numeric value).
- Otherwise: `invalid = -1`.

## Constraints
- Define an unscoped `enum Day`. Write one function that maps a string to its enumerator value (or -1 if no match) and a `constexpr std::string_view` function that maps an enumerator back to its name. Do not use any container types.

test_cases.label: Sample -- weekdays and a weekend day
Hidden -- invalid input mixed with valid
